content.problemStatement: Write a C program that takes an integer as input and determines whether it is an even or odd number. Print "Even" if the number is even and "Odd" if it is odd.
content.inputFormat: First line contains T, the number of test cases. Each test case contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print 'Even' if N is even, otherwise print 'Odd'. Each answer should be on a new line.

hidden.label: stress/general
hidden.expectedOutput:
Even
Odd
Even
Even
Odd
Odd
Even
Odd
Even
Odd

solution.approach: The parity of an integer is determined by its remainder when divided by 2. If N % 2 == 0 the number is even, otherwise it is odd. For each test case we read N, compute N & 1 (bitwise check) or N % 2, and output the corresponding word. This runs in O(1) per test case, total O(T) time, and uses O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) total, O(1) per test case
